title: Velvety Bitter Thai Mushroom Curry
description: A cozy, plantbased Thai curry that balances velvety coconut milk, the subtle bitterness of tamarind and cured shiitake, finished with a splash of dry white wine.

ingredients.name: dried shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: rehydrate in warm water for 15 minutes, then slice

instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat and sauté the minced lemongrass until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the red curry paste and stir to coat the lemongrass, cooking for 2 minutes to release the flavors.
Pour in the white wine, bring to a simmer, and let it reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Add the rehydrated shiitake slices, fresh mushrooms, vegetable broth, coconut milk, tamarind paste, lime zest, and lime leaves; stir gently.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, or until the mushrooms are tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in lime juice, season with salt and pepper to taste, and let the curry rest for 2 minutes off the heat.
Serve hot over steamed jasmine rice or cauliflower rice, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Enjoy the velvety texture and subtle bitterness that lifts each bite.
